confirmed: The Habitat parent candidate-lane README existed at the pinned base and was updated in place.
Bounded repository inspection established the parent README and two child README indexes: ecoregions and habitat_fauna_thin_slice.
Both child indexes explicitly report no child candidate dossier or active candidate.
The Habitat release index says no releases are currently indexed and marks its Habitat Fauna thin-slice row as illustrative rather than a real release.
The domain-habitat workflow retains a recursive non-README candidate detector and explicit validation, proof, and release-readiness holds.
The Habitat proof lane states implementation depth remains UNKNOWN and no accepted Habitat proof producer or inventory was established.
The Habitat domain policy README remains a greenfield scaffold rather than proof of executable policy enforcement.
Current Habitat test and validator surfaces include documentation and placeholder or scaffold modules; accepted suite ownership, pass rate, and detector behavior remain unresolved.
Habitat sensitivity guidance identifies join-induced sensitivity and requires the resulting product to inherit the most restrictive applicable posture.
The shared release-dry-run workflow remains TODO-only in recent observed runs.
proposed: release/candidates/habitat/ should operate as a repository-grounded parent candidate index, governed child-lane router, and deny-by-default pre-publication review contract without becoming Habitat truth, policy, proof, release, or publication authority.
needs_verification: Exhaustive recursive candidate inventory and deterministic candidate-index generation.
Canonical candidate identity, dossier, artifact-manifest, object-family, source-role, model, evidence, sensitivity, public-representation, correction, and rollback contracts.
Accepted parent-versus-child routing and ownership for future Habitat sublanes.
Executable policy, deterministic fixtures, accepted tests and validators, EvidenceBundle resolution, proof production, no-leak enforcement, and catalog closure.
Candidate-to-review, PromotionDecision, ReleaseManifest, correction, withdrawal, supersession, rollback, derivative invalidation, branch-protection, and independent-review topology.

changes: Replaced generic Habitat candidate guidance with a repository-grounded parent candidate index and child-lane router.
Recorded the bounded three-README inventory and no-active-candidate posture.
Indexed the governed ecoregions and habitat_fauna_thin_slice child lanes without treating either README as a candidate.
Preserved the exact A candidate is not a release workflow sentence and publication-not_yet posture.

Added finite candidate states and holds, routing rules, admission fields, Habitat identity and source-role controls, model-observation-regulatory anti-collapse, join-induced sensitivity, geoprivacy and all-surface no-leak rules, time and stale-state discipline, release gates, a public-safe dossier template, validation and automation posture, review handoff, correction and rollback discipline, public-client boundaries, evidence ledger, definition of done, open verification, and documentation rollback.
authority_boundary: This receipt records an AI-authored documentation revision. It is not a Habitat candidate, SourceDescriptor, semantic contract, schema, Habitat observation, model result, sensitive-join decision, geoprivacy transform, EvidenceBundle, PolicyDecision, ReviewRecord, ValidationReport, PromotionDecision, ReleaseManifest, public artifact, CorrectionNotice, WithdrawalNotice, RollbackCard, steward approval, release approval, publication authority, or proof of runtime behavior.

rollback.strategy: Close the draft pull request before merge, or revert the generated-receipt commit and README commit in reverse order after merge.
